1â†’# MMS Fractal Planner - AI Agent Instructions
     2â†’
     3â†’## 1. The Prime Directive
     4â†’
     5â†’You are the Senior GeoView Advisor. You do not write "toy code". You write high-performance, memory-safe, engineering-grade TypeScript.
     6â†’
     7â†’**PROJECT.md is the Single Source of Truth.** If your training data conflicts with PROJECT.md, follow PROJECT.md.
     8â†’
     9â†’## 2. The Harness Protocol
    10â†’
    11â†’Before writing any code, you must execute this loop:
    12â†’
    13â†’1. **OBSERVE:** Run `./init.sh` to see the current state and progress log.
    14â†’2. **PLAN:** Read `features.json`. Select the next `pending` task.
    15â†’3. **THINK:** Write a brief plan in your scratchpad.
    16â†’   - _Example:_ "Implementing SoA. I need `Float64Array` for dates. I will avoid `new Date()` inside the render loop."
    17â†’4. **ACT:** Write the code.
    18â†’5. **VERIFY:** Run `pnpm typecheck` or the relevant test.
    19â†’6. **DOCUMENT:** Update `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json`.
    20â†’
    21â†’## 3. Architecture Constraints (Non-Negotiable)
    22â†’
    23â†’| Feature | Allowed | Forbidden |
    24â†’|---------|---------|-----------|
    25â†’| **Scrolling** | Virtual Scroller (Math-based) | Native DOM Scrolling / Ghost Divs |
    26â†’| **Data Structures** | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (for massive data) |
    27â†’| **State** | Zustand (Transient & Global) | Redux, Context API (for high-freq updates) |
    28â†’| **Rendering** | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM Elements (for >100 items), SVG |
    29â†’| **Loop** | `requestAnimationFrame` | `setInterval`, `setTimeout` (for anim) |
    30â†’
    31â†’## 4. Coding Standards
    32â†’
    33â†’- **Strict Typing:** No `any`. Use `unknown` or Generics if unsure.
    34â†’- **JSDoc:** Required for all exported interfaces and complex algorithms (explain _why_, not just _what_).
    35â†’- **No Comments in Body:** Code should be self-documenting. Use comments only for "Why" or weird math.
    36â†’- **File Structure (TARGET - not yet implemented):**
    37â†’  - `src/engine/`: Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA.
    38â†’  - `src/ui/`: React Components (Overlay, Sidebar).
    39â†’  - `src/render/`: PixiJS specific code (Shaders, Sprites).
    40â†’  - Current legacy: `src/components/`, `src/pages/`, `src/theme/` (to be refactored)
    41â†’
    42â†’## 5. Common Commands
    43â†’
    44â†’```bash
    45â†’# Environment
    46â†’./init.sh             # Check status and architecture compliance
    47â†’pnpm dev              # Vite dev server - REQUIRES index.html & src/main.tsx (not yet created)
    48â†’
    49â†’# Quality
    50â†’pnpm typecheck        # NOT YET CONFIGURED (add to package.json: "tsc --noEmit")
    51â†’pnpm lint             # ESLint (configured)
    52â†’pnpm test             # Vitest - NOT YET INSTALLED
    53â†’
    54â†’# Testing
    55â†’pnpm test:e2e         # Playwright E2E tests (configured)
    56â†’pnpm storybook        # Component documentation (configured)
    57â†’```
    58â†’
    59â†’## 6. Critical Documentation
    60â†’
    61â†’| File | Purpose |
    62â†’|------|---------|
    63â†’| `PROJECT.md` | **SSOT** - Architecture & technical spec |
    64â†’| `features.json` | Task tracking with phases |
    65â†’| `claude-progress.txt` | Session log & next steps |
    66â†’| `docs/RFQ.md` | Client requirements |
    67â†’| `docs/DEMO_DATA.md` | Fleet data, maintenance types |
    68â†’
    69â†’## 7. Performance Targets
    70â†’
    71â†’- **Zoom/Pan:** <16ms response (60fps)
    72â†’- **Initial load:** <3s
    73â†’- **Drag operations:** 60fps constant
    74â†’- **Memory:** No GC pauses during animation
    75â†’- **Data capacity:** 50,000+ schedule blocks
    76â†’
    77â†’## 8. Commit Guidelines
    78â†’
    79â†’- Commit after each significant progress
    80â†’- **NO co-authored-by** in commits
    81â†’- Clear, descriptive messages
    82â†’- Update `claude-progress.txt` after major milestones
    83â†’
    84â†’Example:
    85â†’```
    86â†’feat(engine): implement SoA simulation store
    87â†’
    88â†’- Added TypedArrays for schedule blocks
    89â†’- Implemented Interval Tree for collision detection
    90â†’```
    91â†’

# MMS Fractal Planner - AI Agent Instructions

## 1. The Prime Directive

You are the Senior GeoView Advisor. You do not write "toy code". You write high-performance, memory-safe, engineering-grade TypeScript.

**PROJECT.md is the Single Source of Truth.** If your training data conflicts with PROJECT.md, follow PROJECT.md.

## 2. The Harness Protocol

Before writing any code, you must execute this loop:

1. **OBSERVE:** Run `./init.sh` to see the current state and progress log.
2. **PLAN:** Read `features.json`. Select the next `pending` task.
3. **THINK:** Write a brief plan in your scratchpad.
   - _Example:_ "Implementing SoA. I need `Float64Array` for dates. I will avoid `new Date()` inside the render loop."
4. **ACT:** Write the code.
5. **VERIFY:** Run `pnpm typecheck` or the relevant test.
6. **DOCUMENT:** Update `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json`.

## 3. Architecture Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

| Feature | Allowed | Forbidden |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| **Scrolling** | Virtual Scroller (Math-based) | Native DOM Scrolling / Ghost Divs |
| **Data Structures** | TypedArrays, SoA, Maps | Array of Objects (for massive data) |
| **State** | Zustand (Transient & Global) | Redux, Context API (for high-freq updates) |
| **Rendering** | PixiJS (WebGL), Shaders | DOM Elements (for >100 items), SVG |
| **Loop** | `requestAnimationFrame` | `setInterval`, `setTimeout` (for anim) |

## 4. Coding Standards

- **Strict Typing:** No `any`. Use `unknown` or Generics if unsure.
- **JSDoc:** Required for all exported interfaces and complex algorithms (explain _why_, not just _what_).
- **No Comments in Body:** Code should be self-documenting. Use comments only for "Why" or weird math.
- **File Structure (TARGET - not yet implemented):**
  - `src/engine/`: Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA.
  - `src/ui/`: React Components (Overlay, Sidebar).
  - `src/render/`: PixiJS specific code (Shaders, Sprites).
  - Current legacy: `src/components/`, `src/pages/`, `src/theme/` (to be refactored)

## 5. Common Commands

```bash
# Environment
./init.sh             # Check status and architecture compliance
pnpm dev              # Vite dev server - REQUIRES index.html & src/main.tsx (not yet created)

# Quality
pnpm typecheck        # NOT YET CONFIGURED (add to package.json: "tsc --noEmit")
pnpm lint             # ESLint (configured)
pnpm test             # Vitest - NOT YET INSTALLED

# Testing
pnpm test:e2e         # Playwright E2E tests (configured)
pnpm storybook        # Component documentation (configured)
```

## 6. Critical Documentation

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `PROJECT.md` | **SSOT** - Architecture & technical spec |
| `features.json` | Task tracking with phases |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session log & next steps |
| `docs/RFQ.md` | Client requirements |
| `docs/DEMO_DATA.md` | Fleet data, maintenance types |

## 7. Performance Targets

- **Zoom/Pan:** <16ms response (60fps)
- **Initial load:** <3s
- **Drag operations:** 60fps constant
- **Memory:** No GC pauses during animation
- **Data capacity:** 50,000+ schedule blocks

## 8. Commit Guidelines

- Commit after each significant progress
- **NO co-authored-by** in commits
- Clear, descriptive messages
- Update `claude-progress.txt` after major milestones

Example:
```
feat(engine): implement SoA simulation store

- Added TypedArrays for schedule blocks
- Implemented Interval Tree for collision detection
```

1â†’# Documento de Arquitetura & Especificaأ§أ£o: Rolling Stock Fractal Planner
     2â†’
     3â†’> **Single Source of Truth (SSOT)**
     4â†’>
     5â†’> Este documento consolida a visأ£o tأ©cnica, requisitos funcionais e estratأ©gias de engenharia.
     6â†’>
     7â†’> A implementaأ§أ£o deve aderir estritamente aos princأ­pios de Zero Latency e Data-Oriented Design aqui definidos.
     8â†’
     9â†’---
    10â†’
    11â†’## 1. Visأ£o Tأ©cnica e Princأ­pios
    12â†’
    13â†’O objetivo أ© entregar um **"Single Pane of Glass"** para agendamento de manutenأ§أ£o de **128 trens ao longo de 12 anos**. A performance nأ£o أ© uma "feature", أ© o requisito funcional primأ،rio.
    14â†’
    15â†’**Princأ­pios de Engenharia (Senior Pragmatism):**
    16â†’
    17â†’- **Separation of Concerns (Transient vs. Reactivity):** O React gerencia a أ،rvore de componentes e acessibilidade. O `PixiJS` gerencia a visualizaأ§أ£o de dados. O estado de animaأ§أ£o (drag-and-drop, zoom, pan) أ© **Transiente** (vive fora do ciclo de renderizaأ§أ£o do React) para garantir 60fps.
    18â†’
    19â†’- **Virtual Infinite Scrolling:** Abandonamos o scroll nativo do browser para o eixo temporal. Devido aos limites de precisأ£o de coordenadas do DOM (~33M pixels), implementaremos um **Virtual Scroller** baseado em eventos (`wheel`, `touch`) e inأ©rcia matemأ،tica.
    20â†’
    21â†’- **Data-Oriented Design (DoD):** Para evitar pressأ£o no Garbage Collector (GC), minimizamos a alocaأ§أ£o de objetos. Utilizamos **Structure of Arrays (SoA)** e `TypedArrays` para o nأ؛cleo da simulaأ§أ£o, garantindo _Data Locality_ e cache hits na CPU.
    22â†’
    23â†’- **Heurأ­sticas sobre Perfeiأ§أ£o:** Para interatividade em tempo real (<16ms), utilizamos algoritmos gulosos (_Greedy_) e _Simulated Annealing_ simplificado. A otimizaأ§أ£o perfeita (MIP) أ© reservada para processamento assأ­ncrono.
    24â†’
    25â†’---
    26â†’
    27â†’## 2. Stack Tecnolأ³gico
    28â†’
    29â†’### 2.1 Core Frontend
    30â†’
    31â†’- **Framework:** `React 19` (SPA Mode).
    32â†’- **Build Tool:** `Vite`.
    33â†’- **Language:** `TypeScript 5.x` (Strict Mode).
    34â†’- **State Management (UI/Server):** `Zustand` (Global UI) & `TanStack Query v5` (Async Data).
    35â†’- **State Management (Transient/Canvas):** `Zustand` (Vanilla Store) com assinaturas diretas para evitar re-renders do React durante animaأ§أµes.
    36â†’
    37â†’### 2.2 Renderizaأ§أ£o de Alta Performance (The Canvas)
    38â†’
    39â†’- **Engine:** `PixiJS (v8)`.
    40â†’- **Shader Strategy:** Uso de Custom Fragment Shaders para renderizar Heatmaps (LOD 0) e Grids, movendo a carga da CPU para a GPU.
    41â†’- **Culling:** Implementaأ§أ£o de _View Frustum Culling_ agressivo usando أپrvores de Intervalo (Interval Trees).
    42â†’
    43â†’### 2.3 Camada de Dados & Backend (Simulaأ§أ£o)
    44â†’
    45â†’- **Arquitetura:** Mock Adapter robusto desenhado para substituiأ§أ£o futura por BFF/ODS.
    46â†’- **Determinismo:** O gerador de dados (Mock) deve aceitar uma **Seed** para garantir que cenأ،rios de bugs sejam reproduzأ­veis (nأ£o usar `Math.random()` puro).
    47â†’
    48â†’---
    49â†’
    50â†’## 3. Arquitetura da Aplicaأ§أ£o & Interaأ§أ£o
    51â†’
    52â†’### 3.1 Sistema de Coordenadas & Transformaأ§أµes
    53â†’
    54â†’A conversأ£o entre Tempo (t) e Pixels (x) deve ser linear e de alta precisأ£o (Float64).
    55â†’
    56â†’```
    57â†’x_viewport = (t_unix - t_origin) * S + x_offset
    58â†’```
    59â†’
    60â†’Onde `S` أ© a escala (px/ms) e `x_offset` أ© a translaأ§أ£o virtual.
    61â†’
    62â†’### 3.2 Fractal Viewport (LOD & Histerese)
    63â†’
    64â†’O "Semantic Zoom" altera a visualizaأ§أ£o baseada na escala `S`. Para evitar _flickering_ (mudanأ§a rأ،pida e indesejada de visualizaأ§أ£o) nas bordas dos nأ­veis, implementamos **Histerese**:
    65â†’
    66â†’| Nأ­vel | Escala de Entrada | Escala de Saأ­da | Visualizaأ§أ£o | Tأ©cnica de Render |
    67â†’|-------|-------------------|-----------------|--------------|-------------------|
    68â†’| **LOD 0** | < 0.1 px/h | > 0.15 px/h | Heatmaps (Saturaأ§أ£o) | **Fragment Shader** (GPU) |
    69â†’| **LOD 1** | 0.1 - 20 px/h | < 0.1 ou > 25 px/h | Barras Stacked | Instanced Mesh (PixiJS) |
    70â†’| **LOD 2** | > 20 px/h | < 15 px/h | Gantt / Cأ،psulas | Graphics / Sprites |
    71â†’
    72â†’> **Nota:** As zonas de sobreposiأ§أ£o (ex: entrada em 0.1, saأ­da em 0.15) garantem estabilidade visual.
    73â†’
    74â†’### 3.3 Navigation Strategy (Virtual Scroller)
    75â†’
    76â†’Em vez de um "Ghost Div":
    77â†’
    78â†’1. **Input Capture:** Um container transparente captura eventos `wheel`, `pointerdown`, `pointermove`.
    79â†’2. **Physics Engine:** Um loop de animaأ§أ£o calcula velocidade e atrito (inأ©rcia/momentum).
    80â†’3. **State Update:** Atualiza `viewport.x` no store transiente.
    81â†’4. **Render Trigger:** O PixiJS desenha o frame baseado no novo `viewport.x`.
    82â†’5. **Synchronization:** O React أ© notificado apenas quando o movimento para (idle) ou a cada X ms (throttled) para atualizar a rأ©gua de tempo do DOM.
    83â†’
    84â†’---
    85â†’
    86â†’## 4. O "Constraint Engine" (Simulaأ§أ£o Client-Side)
    87â†’
    88â†’### 4.1 Estrutura de Dados (SoA - Structure of Arrays)
    89â†’
    90â†’Para suportar 50.000+ blocos sem "engasgos" de GC:
    91â†’
    92â†’```typescript
    93â†’// Exemplo conceitual de SoA
    94â†’class SimulationStore {
    95â†’  // Capacidade prأ©-alocada
    96â†’  capacity = 100000;
    97â†’
    98â†’  // TypedArrays: Acesso de memأ³ria contأ­guo e veloz
    99â†’  startTimes = new Float64Array(this.capacity);
   100â†’  endTimes = new Float64Array(this.capacity);
   101â†’  trainIds = new Int32Array(this.capacity); // Mapeado para IDs de string
   102â†’  types = new Uint8Array(this.capacity);    // Enum: 0=PM, 1=Overhaul...
   103â†’
   104â†’  // Spatial Index para buscas rأ،pidas
   105â†’  intervalTree = new FlatIntervalTree(this.startTimes, this.endTimes);
   106â†’}
   107â†’```
   108â†’
   109â†’### 4.2 Game Loop & Validaأ§أ£o
   110â†’
   111â†’1. **Drag Start:** Identifica o أ­ndice do bloco nos Arrays.
   112â†’2. **Move (Tick):**
   113â†’   - Calcula novo `startTime` provisأ³rio.
   114â†’   - **Collision Detection:** Consulta a `IntervalTree` para conflitos no mesmo `trainId` ou `trackId`.
   115â†’   - **Visual Feedback:** Renderiza o bloco em nova posiأ§أ£o (verde/vermelho). **Nأ£o atualiza o React DOM.**
   116â†’3. **Drop (Commit):**
   117â†’   - Aplica a mudanأ§a nos TypedArrays.
   118â†’   - Dispara sincronizaأ§أ£o com o Mock API.
   119â†’   - Atualiza mأ©tricas agregadas.
   120â†’
   121â†’---
   122â†’
   123â†’## 5. Mocking & Testabilidade
   124â†’
   125â†’### 5.1 Mock Adapter Determinأ­stico
   126â†’
   127â†’Para garantir testes de regressأ£o visual e debugging confiأ،vel.
   128â†’
   129â†’```typescript
   130â†’// src/services/mock/generator.ts
   131â†’import seedrandom from 'seedrandom';
   132â†’
   133â†’export class ScenarioGenerator {
   134â†’  private rng: () => number;
   135â†’
   136â†’  constructor(seed: string = 'scen-A-heavy-load') {
   137â†’    this.rng = seedrandom(seed);
   138â†’  }
   139â†’
   140â†’  generateSchedule() {
   141â†’    // Usa this.rng() em vez de Math.random()
   142â†’    // Garante que o cenأ،rio 'scen-A' sempre gere os mesmos conflitos
   143â†’  }
   144â†’}
   145â†’```
   146â†’
   147â†’---
   148â†’
   149â†’## 6. Estrutura de Diretأ³rios
   150â†’
   151â†’```
   152â†’src/
   153â†’â”œâ”€â”€ engine/           # Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA
   154â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ store/        # Zustand stores (transient + global)
   155â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ simulation/   # Constraint solver, TypedArrays
   156â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ physics/      # Virtual scroller, inertia
   157â†’â”œâ”€â”€ render/           # PixiJS specific code
   158â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ shaders/      # Custom GLSL shaders
   159â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ layers/       # LOD renderers (heatmap, bars, capsules)
   160â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ FractalRenderer.ts
   161â†’â”œâ”€â”€ ui/               # React Components (Overlay, Sidebar)
   162â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ components/   # Reusable UI components
   163â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ overlays/     # Tooltips, modals
   164â†’â””â”€â”€ services/         # Mock API, future BFF integration
   165â†’    â””â”€â”€ mock/         # Deterministic data generators
   166â†’```
   167â†’

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

# Documento de Arquitetura & Especificaأ§أ£o: Rolling Stock Fractal Planner

> **Single Source of Truth (SSOT)**
>
> Este documento consolida a visأ£o tأ©cnica, requisitos funcionais e estratأ©gias de engenharia.
>
> A implementaأ§أ£o deve aderir estritamente aos princأ­pios de Zero Latency e Data-Oriented Design aqui definidos.

---

## 1. Visأ£o Tأ©cnica e Princأ­pios

O objetivo أ© entregar um **"Single Pane of Glass"** para agendamento de manutenأ§أ£o de **128 trens ao longo de 12 anos**. A performance nأ£o أ© uma "feature", أ© o requisito funcional primأ،rio.

**Princأ­pios de Engenharia (Senior Pragmatism):**

- **Separation of Concerns (Transient vs. Reactivity):** O React gerencia a أ،rvore de componentes e acessibilidade. O `PixiJS` gerencia a visualizaأ§أ£o de dados. O estado de animaأ§أ£o (drag-and-drop, zoom, pan) أ© **Transiente** (vive fora do ciclo de renderizaأ§أ£o do React) para garantir 60fps.

- **Virtual Infinite Scrolling:** Abandonamos o scroll nativo do browser para o eixo temporal. Devido aos limites de precisأ£o de coordenadas do DOM (~33M pixels), implementaremos um **Virtual Scroller** baseado em eventos (`wheel`, `touch`) e inأ©rcia matemأ،tica.

- **Data-Oriented Design (DoD):** Para evitar pressأ£o no Garbage Collector (GC), minimizamos a alocaأ§أ£o de objetos. Utilizamos **Structure of Arrays (SoA)** e `TypedArrays` para o nأ؛cleo da simulaأ§أ£o, garantindo _Data Locality_ e cache hits na CPU.

- **Heurأ­sticas sobre Perfeiأ§أ£o:** Para interatividade em tempo real (<16ms), utilizamos algoritmos gulosos (_Greedy_) e _Simulated Annealing_ simplificado. A otimizaأ§أ£o perfeita (MIP) أ© reservada para processamento assأ­ncrono.

---

## 2. Stack Tecnolأ³gico

### 2.1 Core Frontend

- **Framework:** `React 19` (SPA Mode).
- **Build Tool:** `Vite`.
- **Language:** `TypeScript 5.x` (Strict Mode).
- **State Management (UI/Server):** `Zustand` (Global UI) & `TanStack Query v5` (Async Data).
- **State Management (Transient/Canvas):** `Zustand` (Vanilla Store) com assinaturas diretas para evitar re-renders do React durante animaأ§أµes.

### 2.2 Renderizaأ§أ£o de Alta Performance (The Canvas)

- **Engine:** `PixiJS (v8)`.
- **Shader Strategy:** Uso de Custom Fragment Shaders para renderizar Heatmaps (LOD 0) e Grids, movendo a carga da CPU para a GPU.
- **Culling:** Implementaأ§أ£o de _View Frustum Culling_ agressivo usando أپrvores de Intervalo (Interval Trees).

### 2.3 Camada de Dados & Backend (Simulaأ§أ£o)

- **Arquitetura:** Mock Adapter robusto desenhado para substituiأ§أ£o futura por BFF/ODS.
- **Determinismo:** O gerador de dados (Mock) deve aceitar uma **Seed** para garantir que cenأ،rios de bugs sejam reproduzأ­veis (nأ£o usar `Math.random()` puro).

---

## 3. Arquitetura da Aplicaأ§أ£o & Interaأ§أ£o

### 3.1 Sistema de Coordenadas & Transformaأ§أµes

A conversأ£o entre Tempo (t) e Pixels (x) deve ser linear e de alta precisأ£o (Float64).

```
x_viewport = (t_unix - t_origin) * S + x_offset
```

Onde `S` أ© a escala (px/ms) e `x_offset` أ© a translaأ§أ£o virtual.

### 3.2 Fractal Viewport (LOD & Histerese)

O "Semantic Zoom" altera a visualizaأ§أ£o baseada na escala `S`. Para evitar _flickering_ (mudanأ§a rأ،pida e indesejada de visualizaأ§أ£o) nas bordas dos nأ­veis, implementamos **Histerese**:

| Nأ­vel | Escala de Entrada | Escala de Saأ­da | Visualizaأ§أ£o | Tأ©cnica de Render |
|-------|-------------------|-----------------|--------------|-------------------|
| **LOD 0** | < 0.1 px/h | > 0.15 px/h | Heatmaps (Saturaأ§أ£o) | **Fragment Shader** (GPU) |
| **LOD 1** | 0.1 - 20 px/h | < 0.1 ou > 25 px/h | Barras Stacked | Instanced Mesh (PixiJS) |
| **LOD 2** | > 20 px/h | < 15 px/h | Gantt / Cأ،psulas | Graphics / Sprites |

> **Nota:** As zonas de sobreposiأ§أ£o (ex: entrada em 0.1, saأ­da em 0.15) garantem estabilidade visual.

### 3.3 Navigation Strategy (Virtual Scroller)

Em vez de um "Ghost Div":

1. **Input Capture:** Um container transparente captura eventos `wheel`, `pointerdown`, `pointermove`.
2. **Physics Engine:** Um loop de animaأ§أ£o calcula velocidade e atrito (inأ©rcia/momentum).
3. **State Update:** Atualiza `viewport.x` no store transiente.
4. **Render Trigger:** O PixiJS desenha o frame baseado no novo `viewport.x`.
5. **Synchronization:** O React أ© notificado apenas quando o movimento para (idle) ou a cada X ms (throttled) para atualizar a rأ©gua de tempo do DOM.

---

## 4. O "Constraint Engine" (Simulaأ§أ£o Client-Side)

### 4.1 Estrutura de Dados (SoA - Structure of Arrays)

Para suportar 50.000+ blocos sem "engasgos" de GC:

```typescript
// Exemplo conceitual de SoA
class SimulationStore {
  // Capacidade prأ©-alocada
  capacity = 100000;

  // TypedArrays: Acesso de memأ³ria contأ­guo e veloz
  startTimes = new Float64Array(this.capacity);
  endTimes = new Float64Array(this.capacity);
  trainIds = new Int32Array(this.capacity); // Mapeado para IDs de string
  types = new Uint8Array(this.capacity);    // Enum: 0=PM, 1=Overhaul...

  // Spatial Index para buscas rأ،pidas
  intervalTree = new FlatIntervalTree(this.startTimes, this.endTimes);
}
```

### 4.2 Game Loop & Validaأ§أ£o

1. **Drag Start:** Identifica o أ­ndice do bloco nos Arrays.
2. **Move (Tick):**
   - Calcula novo `startTime` provisأ³rio.
   - **Collision Detection:** Consulta a `IntervalTree` para conflitos no mesmo `trainId` ou `trackId`.
   - **Visual Feedback:** Renderiza o bloco em nova posiأ§أ£o (verde/vermelho). **Nأ£o atualiza o React DOM.**
3. **Drop (Commit):**
   - Aplica a mudanأ§a nos TypedArrays.
   - Dispara sincronizaأ§أ£o com o Mock API.
   - Atualiza mأ©tricas agregadas.

---

## 5. Mocking & Testabilidade

### 5.1 Mock Adapter Determinأ­stico

Para garantir testes de regressأ£o visual e debugging confiأ،vel.

```typescript
// src/services/mock/generator.ts
import seedrandom from 'seedrandom';

export class ScenarioGenerator {
  private rng: () => number;

  constructor(seed: string = 'scen-A-heavy-load') {
    this.rng = seedrandom(seed);
  }

  generateSchedule() {
    // Usa this.rng() em vez de Math.random()
    // Garante que o cenأ،rio 'scen-A' sempre gere os mesmos conflitos
  }
}
```

---

## 6. Estrutura de Diretأ³rios

```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ engine/           # Core logic, Physics, Math, SoA
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ store/        # Zustand stores (transient + global)
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ simulation/   # Constraint solver, TypedArrays
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ physics/      # Virtual scroller, inertia
â”œâ”€â”€ render/           # PixiJS specific code
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ shaders/      # Custom GLSL shaders
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ layers/       # LOD renderers (heatmap, bars, capsules)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ FractalRenderer.ts
â”œâ”€â”€ ui/               # React Components (Overlay, Sidebar)
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ components/   # Reusable UI components
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ overlays/     # Tooltips, modals
â””â”€â”€ services/         # Mock API, future BFF integration
    â””â”€â”€ mock/         # Deterministic data generators
```

# MMS Fractal Planner - Agent State Log

## Context & Directives

Current Phase: Architectural Pivot (Redux/Astro -> PixiJS/Vite/SoA)
Role: Senior Frontend Engineer (Performance Specialist)
Constraint: Strict adherence to PROJECT.md (No "Ghost Divs", No Object-heavy state)

## Session Log

### Session 1-4 (Legacy) - 2025-12-11/12

**Status:** Completed initial setup (Redux/Astro) - _DEPRECATED_

- Note: Previous work on Redux/Astro is now obsolete. We are refactoring to a high-performance architecture.
- Setup-001 to setup-008 were completed but are being replaced.

---

### Session 5 - 2025-12-12 (Architectural Rewrite)

**Goal:** Establish the new "Senior Advisor" harness and refactor the base.

**Completed:**
- [x] Updated PROJECT.md with new SoA/Virtual Scroller architecture
- [x] Updated features.json with new task structure
- [x] Updated claude-progress.txt (this file)
- [x] Updated CLAUDE.md with Senior Advisor instructions
- [x] Updated init.sh with architecture compliance checks

**NOT Completed (claimed but not done):**
- [ ] `src/pages/index.astro` still exists - needs removal
- [ ] Old component structure still exists (`src/components/`, `src/pages/`, `src/theme/`)
- [ ] New directory structure not created (`src/engine/`, `src/ui/`, `src/render/`)

---

### Session 6 - 2025-12-12 (Documentation Sync)

**Goal:** Sync documentation with actual codebase state.

**Completed:**
- [x] Audited actual file structure vs documented structure
- [x] Updated claude-progress.txt with accurate state

---

## CURRENT STATE (Verified 2025-12-12)

### Critical Missing Files (Vite SPA not functional):
- â‌Œ `index.html` (root) - Required for Vite SPA entry point
- â‌Œ `src/main.tsx` - React entry point
- â‌Œ `src/App.tsx` - Root React component

### Actual File Structure:
```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ components/       # HelloReact.tsx, HelloReact.stories.tsx
â”œâ”€â”€ pages/            # index.astro (LEGACY - needs removal)
â””â”€â”€ theme/            # theme.ts, ThemeProvider.tsx
```

### Target Structure (per PROJECT.md):
```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ engine/           # NOT YET CREATED
â”œâ”€â”€ render/           # NOT YET CREATED
â”œâ”€â”€ ui/               # NOT YET CREATED
â””â”€â”€ services/         # NOT YET CREATED
```

### Configuration Files:
- âœ… `vite.config.ts` - Configured with React plugin and @ alias
- âœ… `eslint.config.js` - ESLint 9 flat config
- âœ… `playwright.config.ts` - E2E testing

### Dependencies Installed:
- âœ… React 19, PixiJS v8, Zustand v5, MUI v7
- âœ… D3-scale, D3-time (for time axis calculations)
- â‌Œ TanStack Query (not installed)
- â‌Œ seedrandom (not installed)
- â‌Œ Vitest (not installed, only Playwright for E2E)

### Scripts Available:
- âœ… `pnpm dev` - Vite dev server (but won't work without index.html)
- âœ… `pnpm lint` - ESLint
- âœ… `pnpm storybook` - Component documentation
- âœ… `pnpm test:e2e` - Playwright E2E tests
- â‌Œ `pnpm typecheck` - NOT CONFIGURED (no script in package.json)

---

## NEXT STEPS FOR AGENT

1. **Execute:** `refactor-001` - Remove `src/pages/index.astro`, create Vite SPA entry point
2. **Execute:** `refactor-002` - Install TanStack Query, seedrandom, vitest
3. **Execute:** `refactor-003` - Create new directory structure per PROJECT.md
4. **Implement:** Create `src/engine/store/` with Zustand stores
5. **Update:** Mark tasks as done in `features.json` and log here

---

## Uncertainties / Risks

- Ensure Node.js memory limit is sufficient if testing 12 years of generated data immediately
- Browser WebGL context loss handling needs to be considered later
- TypedArray size limits for very large datasets (>100k blocks)

---

## Architecture Notes

Key files to create:
- `src/engine/store/viewportStore.ts` - Transient canvas state (Zustand vanilla)
- `src/engine/store/simulationStore.ts` - SoA data structures
- `src/engine/physics/virtualScroller.ts` - Inertia/momentum calculations
- `src/render/FractalRenderer.ts` - PixiJS entry point

  },

    "@mui/icons-material": "^7.3.6",

    "d3-scale": "^4.0.2",
    "d3-time": "^3.1.0",
    "pixi.js": "^8.0.0",
    "react": "^19.2.3",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.3",
    "zustand": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.1",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.57.0",
    "@storybook/addon-essentials": "^8.6.14",
    "@storybook/blocks": "^8.6.14",
    "@storybook/react-vite": "^8.6.14",
    "@storybook/test": "^8.6.14",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "eslint": "^9.39.1",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.8",
    "eslint-plugin-react": "^7.37.5",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.0.1",

  }

     2â†’  "project": "mms-fractal-planner",
     3â†’  "version": "1.0.0-rewrite",
     4â†’  "description": "High-Performance Train Maintenance Planner (PixiJS + SoA)",
     5â†’  "stack": {
     6â†’    "framework": "Vite",
     7â†’    "ui": ["React 19", "MUI v7"],
     8â†’    "rendering": "PixiJS v8",
     9â†’    "state": ["Zustand (UI)", "Zustand Vanilla (Transient)", "TanStack Query"],
    10â†’    "language": "TypeScript 5.x (Strict)",
    11â†’    "testing": ["Vitest", "Playwright"],
    12â†’    "architecture": "Data-Oriented Design (SoA)"
    13â†’  },
    14â†’  "phases": {
    15â†’    "setup-refactor": [
    16â†’      { "id": "refactor-001", "name": "Remove Astro & Redux dependencies", "status": "pending" },
    17â†’      { "id": "refactor-002", "name": "Install TanStack Query & seedrandom", "status": "pending" },
    18â†’      { "id": "refactor-003", "name": "Configure Vite SPA mode", "status": "pending" }
    19â†’    ],
    20â†’    "core-engine": [
    21â†’      { "id": "engine-001", "name": "Implement SoA Data Structures (TypedArrays)", "status": "pending" },
    22â†’      { "id": "engine-002", "name": "Deterministic Mock Generator (Seeded)", "status": "pending" },
    23â†’      { "id": "engine-003", "name": "Virtual Scroller Logic (Physics/Inertia)", "status": "pending" },
    24â†’      { "id": "engine-004", "name": "Coordinate System Transformer (Time <-> Pixels)", "status": "pending" }
    25â†’    ],
    26â†’    "rendering-layers": [
    27â†’      { "id": "render-001", "name": "PixiJS Stage & Context Integration", "status": "pending" },
    28â†’      { "id": "render-002", "name": "LOD 0: Heatmap Fragment Shader", "status": "pending" },
    29â†’      { "id": "render-003", "name": "LOD 1: Instanced Mesh for Bars", "status": "pending" },
    30â†’      { "id": "render-004", "name": "LOD 2: Capsule Sprites & Text", "status": "pending" },
    31â†’      { "id": "render-005", "name": "Hysteresis Zoom Logic", "status": "pending" }
    32â†’    ],
    33â†’    "interaction": [
